mission.title: openeverest: OIDC does not work with Azure AD — internal error after login
mission.description: OIDC authentication with Azure AD allows login (user name is shown) but the Everest interface displays an internal error. This affects users configuring Azure AD as their OIDC provider.

steps.title: Identify the OIDC error
steps.description:
Check if the Everest UI shows an internal error after OIDC login:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=100 | grep -i oidc
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for token validation errors or scope-related issues.

steps.title: Review OIDC configuration
steps.description:
Check the current OIDC settings:
```bash
kubectl get configmap everest-settings -n everest-system -o yaml
```

For Azure AD, the configuration should look like:
```
everestctl settings oidc configure \
    --issuer-url="https://login.microsoftonline.com/<TENANT_ID>/v2.0" \
    --client-id="<APP_CLIENT_ID>" \
    --scopes="openid,profile,email"
```

Note: Avoid using `<app-id>/.default` scope as it can cause issues with token validation.

steps.title: Verify Azure AD app registration
steps.description:
In the Azure portal, verify your app registration:
1. Ensure the app is registered as a SPA (Single Page Application)
2. Redirect URIs include your Everest URL
3. Token configuration includes 'email' optional claim
4. API permissions include: openid, profile, email

Check that the token contains the expected claims:
```bash
# Decode the JWT token to inspect claims
echo '<token>' | cut -d'.' -f2 | base64 -d 2>/dev/null | jq .
```

steps.title: Confirm OIDC is working
steps.description:
After fixing the configuration, verify OIDC login works end-to-end:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=50 --since=5m | grep -i oidc
```
Confirm there are no more error messages after login.

resolution.summary: OIDC with Azure AD fails because of scope configuration or missing token claims. Ensure the Azure AD app registration is configured as a SPA with the correct redirect URIs and that the OIDC scopes are set to 'openid,profile,email' without the .default scope.
codeSnippets: kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=100 | grep -i oidc

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with OpenEverest installed and Azure AD configured as OIDC provider.
